step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("anatomical origin of anterior portion of tongue")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the newly retrieved evidence, particularly points [9], [13], and [15]:
Points [9] and [15]: Emphasize that the lateral lingual swellings and tuberculum impar merge to form the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. Point [13] supports this notion by detailing that the anterior two-thirds get their motor innervation exclusively from the hypoglossal nerve and their sensory innervation from the lingual nerve, confirming their developmental derivation uniquely tied to these early growth areas.
Given this confirmation alongside our previous reasoning, I reaffirm agreement with Option B - the anterior two-thirds of the tongue being correctly attributed to the contributions of the lateral lingual swellings and tuberculum impar.
